steps.title: Read what the graph shows for September
steps.content: For September, the four Puerto Rican cities split into two groups: Rincon, Ponce, and Isabela cluster at relatively low rainfall values (each under 8 inches), while San Sebastian sits noticeably higher, above 8 inches.

steps.title: Match a choice to that pattern
steps.content: The correct option must use the September data to capture this split between the three lower cities and the one higher city.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Rincón's average rainfall is greater than that of Isabela, San Sebastián, and Ponce.: misreads the data — Rincon's September rainfall isn't greater than all three other cities; San Sebastian is higher. ✗
  • Choice B — Rincón and Ponce have an average rainfall of about 5 inches, and Isabela and San Sebastián have an average rainfall of about 10 inches.: wrong pairing and wrong values — Rincon and Ponce aren't both around 5, and Isabela isn't around 10 (San Sebastian is the high one). ✗
  • Choice C — Rincón, Ponce, and Isabela each have an average rainfall below 8 inches, but San Sebastián's average rainfall that month is greater than 8 inches.: correctly reports the three-versus-one split visible in the September data. ✓
  • Choice D — Rincón has a similar average rainfall to Isabela, and Ponce has a similar average rainfall to San Sebastián.: false comparison — Ponce and San Sebastian are far apart in September, not similar. ✗
That matches Choice C — Rincón, Ponce, and Isabela each have an average rainfall below 8 inches, but San Sebastián's average rainfall that month is greater than 8 inches..
